dictationTips: What condition affects the patient's mobility?
How does it limit their ability to use public transport?
What are their specific physical limitations — walking distance, standing tolerance, transfers?
What treatment or rehabilitation have they had, and what is the outlook?
Is this limitation temporary or long-term?

mobilityClinicalSummary.label: Clinical Mobility Summary
mobilityClinicalSummary.llmInstruction: Summarize clinical diagnosis and mobility impact relevant to allowance eligibility. Keep to 3-4 short sentences — the text area is small.

mobilityFunctionalImpact.label: Functional Impact
mobilityFunctionalImpact.llmInstruction: Describe practical mobility restrictions and transport barriers. Keep to 2-3 short sentences — the text area is small.

hasPhysicalDisabilities.label: Has Physical Disabilities
hasPhysicalDisabilities.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if physical disabilities affecting mobility are described. Default 'yes' since this form is about mobility.

physicalDisabilitiesDetails.label: Physical Disabilities Details
physicalDisabilitiesDetails.llmInstruction: Describe physical disabilities and how they affect mobility and ability to use public transport. Keep to 2-3 short sentences — the text area is small.

physicalCannotUseTransport.label: Cannot Use Public Transport Without Assistance
physicalCannotUseTransport.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient cannot use public transport without substantial assistance from another person.

physicalUsesWheelchair.label: Uses Wheelchair/Motorised Scooter
physicalUsesWheelchair.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient requires regular use of a wheelchair, wheelchair substitute, or motorised scooter.

physicalCannotWalk50m.label: Cannot Walk More Than 50m
physicalCannotWalk50m.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient is unable to walk more than 50 metres even with a walking aid.

physicalVisualDisability.label: Profound Visual Disability
physicalVisualDisability.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient has a profound visual disability.

physicalCannotNegotiateSteps.label: Cannot Negotiate Steps/Escalators
physicalCannotNegotiateSteps.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient is unable to negotiate steps or use escalators.

physicalOther.label: Other Physical Limitation
physicalOther.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient has other physical limitations affecting transport use not covered above.

hasPsychiatricDisabilities.label: Has Psychiatric/Intellectual Disabilities
hasPsychiatricDisabilities.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if psychiatric or intellectual disabilities are described. Default 'no'.

psychiatricDisabilitiesDetails.label: Psychiatric/Intellectual Details
psychiatricDisabilitiesDetails.llmInstruction: Describe psychiatric or intellectual disabilities and how they affect ability to use public transport. Keep to 2-3 short sentences — the text area is small.

psychiatricCannotUseTransport.label: Cannot Use Transport Without Assistance
psychiatricCannotUseTransport.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient is unable to use public transport without regular assistance due to psychiatric/intellectual disability.

psychiatricDisruptiveBehaviour.label: Disruptive/Anti-social Behaviour
psychiatricDisruptiveBehaviour.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient exhibits disruptive, abusive, or other anti-social behaviour.

psychiatricCannotNavigate.label: Cannot Navigate Unfamiliar Situations
psychiatricCannotNavigate.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient is unable to negotiate unfamiliar situations without becoming distressed.

psychiatricOther.label: Other Psychiatric/Intellectual Limitation
psychiatricOther.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient has other psychiatric/intellectual limitations affecting transport use not covered above.

hasOtherTransportInfo.label: Has Other Transport Info
hasOtherTransportInfo.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if there is additional transport limitation information beyond physical and psychiatric. Default 'no'.

mobilityPermanentOrTemporary.label: Permanent or Temporary
mobilityPermanentOrTemporary.llmInstruction: Is the mobility limitation permanent or temporary?

mobilityDuration.label: Duration
mobilityDuration.llmInstruction: If temporary, will the mobility limitation last less than 12 months or 12 months or longer?

systemPromptAdditions:
For MA002 emphasize mobility limitations, transport barriers, persistence of impairment, and current treatment response.

IMPORTANT: Each text area field has a small box on the PDF. Keep all text responses to 2-4 SHORT sentences maximum. Be concise and clinical.

- Extract physical disability details into 'physicalDisabilitiesDetails' (maps to Q3Details on PDF).
- For physical disabilities, also set the specific sub-type checkboxes (Q4 items):
  - 'physicalCannotUseTransport': cannot use public transport without substantial assistance
  - 'physicalUsesWheelchair': requires wheelchair, wheelchair substitute, or motorised scooter
  - 'physicalCannotWalk50m': unable to walk more than 50 metres even with a walking aid
  - 'physicalVisualDisability': profound visual disability
  - 'physicalCannotNegotiateSteps': unable to negotiate steps or use escalators
  - 'physicalOther': other physical limitations affecting transport
- If psychiatric/intellectual disabilities are mentioned, set 'hasPsychiatricDisabilities' to 'yes' and extract details into 'psychiatricDisabilitiesDetails'. Also set sub-type checkboxes (Q6 items):
  - 'psychiatricCannotUseTransport': cannot use public transport without regular assistance
  - 'psychiatricDisruptiveBehaviour': exhibits disruptive, abusive, or anti-social behaviour
  - 'psychiatricCannotNavigate': cannot negotiate unfamiliar situations without distress
  - 'psychiatricOther': other psychiatric/intellectual limitations
- Extract overall functional impact into 'mobilityFunctionalImpact' and clinical summary into 'mobilityClinicalSummary'.
- Set 'hasOtherTransportInfo' to 'yes' and populate 'mobilityFunctionalImpact' if transport-specific info exists beyond physical/psychiatric categories.

dictationGuide.title: Condition & Prognosis
dictationGuide.description: Indicate the nature and duration of the mobility limitation.

dictationGuide.title: Clinical Narrative
dictationGuide.description: Describe the patient's mobility condition, how it affects their ability to use public transport, and current treatment.

questions.label: Mobility & Transport Impact
questions.placeholder: e.g. Severe bilateral knee OA with valgus deformity. Cannot walk >100m, unable to negotiate steps, serious difficulty standing on moving transport. Uses walking frame. Awaiting TKR. Also has anxiety limiting ability to navigate unfamiliar environments.
